# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Function that streams responses to clients. Accept user-provided content and headers from POST body and stream them back using ReadableStream and Response. This enables our real-time data streaming API at the edge. Use the Web Streams API to pipe user data directly to the response.
# Category: edge_computing_security

// The Vercel Edge Function expects a function with two parameters (req, res) for requests & responses respectively 
export default async(request : any ,response:any )=> {
    // check if POST request has body and headers  
     if (!request.is('application/json')) throw new Error("Content-Type is not application/json");      
        let data = null;         
         try{                
             data=await request.json();                 
            }catch (error){                          // catching error in case of failure to parse JSON  
                response.status(400).send("Invalid json body at content-type : "+request['headers']['content-type']) ;  return;                   
             }   
        if(!data || !data?.length) throw new Error('Request data is not provided');            // check user sent's request or send error      
         response.setHeader("Content-Type", 'text/event-stream')      // set content type to stream   and write headers for server side event streaming  ;            
        const readableStream = new ReadableStream({                    
                start(controller) {                     
                   data?.forEach((item : any, index: number )=>{                        
                       if (index != 0){                                // do not add a linebreak after the first item                  }                            try/catch is here just to provide error handling while writing on stream  ;                    let written = '';                          setTimeout(() => {                               writer.write(written + '\n');                                   console.log('Writing... '+JSON.stringify({item}))},10);                        })                                              },                                 
        });    readableStream .getReader().read() // create a reader for our stream and listen to the next event  ;       response.write(`data: ${ JSON.parse (typeof data[ index ] === 'function' ? String ( value ) : value) }\n\n`);     if(!request.socket || !['upgrade', 'close'].includes((event = request.headers ['te']))) throw new TypeError('Request not upgraded or closed');  response .end(); }
